_comment: Track Record v4.1 -- RANK-based call semantics, tiers v3. A 'call' is the TOP k / BOTTOM k of the creator's GRADED WINDOW, ranked by stored prediction (y_pred) DESC with a deterministic tie-break (posted_at DESC, then id DESC), so both surfaces rank identically. GRADED WINDOW = the `gradedWindow` (40) most-recent graded videos by posted_at: it expands until 40, then ROLLS -- older rows leave the display (their times_typical stays frozen; call_type/verdict re-rank live over the current window). k from sizeTiers by the in-window graded count n: n in 6-8 -> k=2, 9-11 -> k=3, 12-40 -> k=4; n<6 -> no calls at all (floor raised from 4 in v4 -> 6 in v4.1). At the full window k=4/40 = the top/bottom DECILE -- the study's own top-vs-bottom measurement construction. TOP-k rows are 'strong' calls, BOTTOM-k 'weak', the rest 'none' (a no-call: displayed, never scored hit/miss). Verdict from the frozen result (times_typical = result / creator-median): a strong call hits iff times_typical >= 1.0, a weak call iff < 1.0. `callsTier` / `averagesTier` are the shared impressiveness-tier boundaries for the ADAPTIVE HERO (Option A): callsTier by the fraction C_hits/2k (>=5/6 -> 3, >=2/3 -> 2, >=1/2 -> 1, else 0), averagesTier by the gap top_avg-bottom_avg. The hero leads with whichever tier is higher (tie -> averages). Consumed by BOTH server.js (grading pass + /api/track-record hero) and generate_preview.py (PDF). Never hardcode the tiers/window/boundaries anywhere else -- read this file. See TRACK_RECORD_V41_READOUT.md for the tier/floor migration.
